1	# UAT Template
2	
3	Template for `.planning/phases/XX-name/{phase_num}-UAT.md` — persistent UAT session tracking.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## File Template
8	
9	```markdown
10	---
11	status: testing | partial | complete | diagnosed
12	phase: XX-name
13	source: [list of SUMMARY.md files tested]
14	started: [ISO timestamp]
15	updated: [ISO timestamp]
16	---
17	
18	## Current Test
19	<!-- OVERWRITE each test - shows where we are -->
20	
21	number: [N]
22	name: [test name]
23	expected: |
24	  [what user should observe]
25	awaiting: user response
26	
27	## Tests
28	
29	### 1. [Test Name]
30	expected: [observable behavior - what user should see]
31	result: [pending]
32	
33	### 2. [Test Name]
34	expected: [observable behavior]
35	result: pass
36	
37	### 3. [Test Name]
38	expected: [observable behavior]
39	result: issue
40	reported: "[verbatim user response]"
41	severity: major
42	
43	### 4. [Test Name]
44	expected: [observable behavior]
45	result: skipped
46	reason: [why skipped]
47	
48	### 5. [Test Name]
49	expected: [observable behavior]
50	result: blocked
51	blocked_by: server | physical-device | release-build | third-party | prior-phase
52	reason: [why blocked]
53	
54	...
55	
56	## Summary
57	
58	total: [N]
59	passed: [N]
60	issues: [N]
61	pending: [N]
62	skipped: [N]
63	blocked: [N]
64	
65	## Gaps
66	
67	<!-- YAML format for plan-phase --gaps consumption -->
68	- truth: "[expected behavior from test]"
69	  status: failed
70	  reason: "User reported: [verbatim response]"
71	  severity: blocker | major | minor | cosmetic
72	  test: [N]
73	  root_cause: ""     # Filled by diagnosis
74	  artifacts: []      # Filled by diagnosis
75	  missing: []        # Filled by diagnosis
76	  debug_session: ""  # Filled by diagnosis
77	```
78	
79	---
80	
81	<section_rules>
82	
83	**Frontmatter:**
84	- `status`: OVERWRITE - "testing", "partial", or "complete"
85	- `phase`: IMMUTABLE - set on creation
86	- `source`: IMMUTABLE - SUMMARY files being tested
87	- `started`: IMMUTABLE - set on creation
88	- `updated`: OVERWRITE - update on every change
89	
90	**Current Test:**
91	- OVERWRITE entirely on each test transition
92	- Shows which test is active and what's awaited
93	- On completion: "[testing complete]"
94	
95	**Tests:**
96	- Each test: OVERWRITE result field when user responds
97	- `result` values: [pending], pass, issue, skipped, blocked
98	- If issue: add `reported` (verbatim) and `severity` (inferred)
99	- If skipped: add `reason` if provided
100	- If blocked: add `blocked_by` (tag) and `reason` (if provided)
101	
102	**Summary:**
103	- OVERWRITE counts after each response
104	- Tracks: total, passed, issues, pending, skipped
105	
106	**Gaps:**
107	- APPEND only when issue found (YAML format)
108	- After diagnosis: fill `root_cause`, `artifacts`, `missing`, `debug_session`
109	- This section feeds directly into /gsd-plan-phase --gaps
110	
111	</section_rules>
112	
113	<diagnosis_lifecycle>
114	
115	**After testing complete (status: complete), if gaps exist:**
116	
117	1. User runs diagnosis (from verify-work offer or manually)
118	2. diagnose-issues workflow spawns parallel debug agents
119	3. Each agent investigates one gap, returns root cause
120	4. UAT.md Gaps section updated with diagnosis:
121	   - Each gap gets `root_cause`, `artifacts`, `missing`, `debug_session` filled
122	5. status → "diagnosed"
123	6. Ready for /gsd-plan-phase --gaps with root causes
124	
125	**After diagnosis:**
126	```yaml
127	## Gaps
128	
129	- truth: "Comment appears immediately after submission"
130	  status: failed
131	  reason: "User reported: works but doesn't show until I refresh the page"
132	  severity: major
133	  test: 2
134	  root_cause: "useEffect in CommentList.tsx missing commentCount dependency"
135	  artifacts:
136	    - path: "src/components/CommentList.tsx"
137	      issue: "useEffect missing dependency"
138	  missing:
139	    - "Add commentCount to useEffect dependency array"
140	  debug_session: ".planning/debug/comment-not-refreshing.md"
141	```
142	
143	</diagnosis_lifecycle>
144	
145	<lifecycle>
146	
147	**Creation:** When /gsd-verify-work starts new session
148	- Extract tests from SUMMARY.md files
149	- Set status to "testing"
150	- Current Test points to test 1
151	- All tests have result: [pending]
152	
153	**During testing:**
154	- Present test from Current Test section
155	- User responds with pass confirmation or issue description
156	- Update test result (pass/issue/skipped)
157	- Update Summary counts
158	- If issue: append to Gaps section (YAML format), infer severity
159	- Move Current Test to next pending test
160	
161	**On completion:**
162	- status → "complete"
163	- Current Test → "[testing complete]"
164	- Commit file
165	- Present summary with next steps
166	
167	**Partial completion:**
168	- status → "partial" (if pending, blocked, or unresolved skipped tests remain)
169	- Current Test → "[testing paused — {N} items outstanding]"
170	- Commit file
171	- Present summary with outstanding items highlighted
172	
173	**Resuming partial session:**
174	- `/gsd-verify-work {phase}` picks up from first pending/blocked test
175	- When all items resolved, status advances to "complete"
176	
177	**Resume after /clear:**
178	1. Read frontmatter → know phase and status
179	2. Read Current Test → know where we are
180	3. Find first [pending] result → continue from there
181	4. Summary shows progress so far
182	
183	</lifecycle>
184	
185	<severity_guide>
186	
187	Severity is INFERRED from user's natural language, never asked.
188	
189	| User describes | Infer |
190	|----------------|-------|
191	| Crash, error, exception, fails completely, unusable | blocker |
192	| Doesn't work, nothing happens, wrong behavior, missing | major |
193	| Works but..., slow, weird, minor, small issue | minor |
194	| Color, font, spacing, alignment, visual, looks off | cosmetic |
195	
196	Default: **major** (safe default, user can clarify if wrong)
197	
198	</severity_guide>
199

10	Phase 01 (folder-sync MVP) on branch `01-folder-sync`. GSD state `human_needed`; close with `/gsd-verify-work 01` once the open finding below is resolved or explicitly deferred.
11	
12	Re-UAT (through 2026-07-11): all 8 original UAT tests pass (incl. Test 8 VM integration suite, 57 green). A gap-closure round fixed findings + added features, each released and hand-confirmed on the VMs. Confirmed on a real terminal:
13	- confirm-boundary TUI overwrite → resume on a FRESH Live below the printed prompt (a reused Live kept its pre-pause cursor shape and repainted over the warning) [alpha.16]
14	- job failure logged "unhandled errors in a TaskGroup" → `_unwrap_taskgroup_error` surfaces the real leaf cause [alpha.17]
15	- duplicate CRITICAL job-failure log → per-job handler marks the exc via `_mark_failure_logged`; run()'s top-level `except Exception` skips already-logged causes (kept for non-job phases + future parallel-job failures) [alpha.18]
16	- invalid GITHUB_TOKEN hard-failed the sync at install-on-target → `get_releases` warns + falls back to unauthenticated on `BadCredentialsException` [alpha.19]
17	- bare "Step N/M" → `set_current_step(step, name)`; orchestrator labels each phase + job [alpha.20]
18	
19	Latest release **v0.1.0-alpha.20** (HEAD `b4234a0`). Release rule (relearned the hard way): cut a release ONLY after CI incl. the VM integration suite is green on that exact commit — see [[pcswitcher-release-annotated-tags]].
20	
21	OPEN finding (NOT fixed) — warnings not reliably visible: a WARNING (e.g. the token-rejected one) shows only in the ephemeral Recent Logs panel (a rolling 10-line Live region, erased when a prompt pauses the Live), so the config-sync prompt overwrites it and the user misses it; they won't open the log file every run. Needs a persistent indicator + a way to view warnings. Proposed: buffer ≥WARNING records during the run → persistent "⚠ N" counter in the status bar + a static end-of-run summary that lists them (survives in scrollback, printed after the Live stops) with a `pc-switcher logs` pointer. To be designed in a fresh session.
22	
23	Pending wrap-up (after the open finding is resolved/deferred): user reverts dummy_success/dummy_fail from the VM config (their file on pc1); update `01-UAT.md` with the gap-closure findings + fixes; run `/gsd-verify-work 01`.
24	
25	Key facts:
26	- Test VMs (Hetzner, persistent, billed): pc1 `91.99.178.190`, pc2 `138.199.146.229`, user `testuser`; hcloud token `pass show dev/pc-switcher/testing/hcloud_token_rw`. VMs are reset by restoring btrfs BASELINE snapshots (`reset-vm.sh`), so live edits to VM files do NOT persist; provisioning is CI-only.
27	- Valid GitHub token lives at `pass show dev/pc-switcher/github_token_public_ro` (r/o public; 200 on the `/repos/.../releases` endpoint that pc-switcher calls — test that endpoint, NOT `/user`, which a `ghs_` token can't access). The VMs' `~/.profile` currently holds a stale ephemeral CI `ghs_` token (injected by `conftest.set_github_token_env_var` from CI's `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN`, only-if-absent guard → never refreshed → rots). User is resolving this separately — do NOT re-diagnose or file it.
28	- install_on_target pulls branch code from a git tag → cut an ANNOTATED pre-release tag via the git data API (after CI green) per [[pcswitcher-release-annotated-tags]]. uv's git cache on the persistent VMs masks a new tag → `uv cache clean` before reinstall, else `--version` reads `<prev-tag>+post.N.dev`.
29	- Branch added: ADR-015 topology safety; first-sync split from out-of-order (`--allow-first-sync` vs `--allow-out-of-order`); reusable `Confirmer`; rsync-over-SSH root-creds fix.
30	
31	Extends [[pcswitcher-status-and-mvp-direction]].

22	## Tests
23	
24	Scope: this UAT covers only what automation cannot — the interactive UX and a whole-flow walkthrough for maintainer confidence. The data-level guarantees (byte-identical content, numeric uid/gid, permissions incl. special bits, ACLs, mtime, hard/sym links across user/root/other-user files and dirs, config exclusions, ADR-016 runtime-file exclusion, directory-deletion propagation, the non-interactive `--yes`/`--allow-first-sync`/`--allow-out-of-order` paths) are covered by the automated suite and are proven by Test 8 (the VM integration run). This is a fresh restart re-testing against the fixed code from gap-closure round 01-15..01-18 + code-review.
25	
26	### 1. Install pc-switcher on machine A (first-run install UX)
27	
28	expected: Following the documented install path (install script / `uv tool install` from a release), pc-switcher installs on a real machine A. `pc-switcher --version` reports the installed version; `pc-switcher init` creates `~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml` with the default `/home` + `/root` folder-sync config. Docs are sufficient to complete the install without guessing.
29	
30	why_human: Validates the real install experience and documentation on a real machine — a UX/docs check, not a scriptable assertion.
31	
32	result: pass
33	
34	note: Installed on clean pc1 via `curl .../refs/tags/v0.1.0-alpha.14/install.sh | VERSION=v0.1.0-alpha.14 bash`; `pc-switcher --version` → 0.1.0-alpha.14 (clean release tag); `init` created config.yaml. Release must be an ANNOTATED tag (see issue #161 / memory) — lightweight tags install as a wrong dev version. Known first-run UX gap: installer puts binary in ~/.local/bin without adding it to the current shell or telling the user (fresh login shell fixes it) — user accepted Test 1 as pass.
35	
36	### 2. First-sync warning — abort (interactive, fixed code)
37	
38	expected: With target B having no prior sync history, run `pc-switcher sync <B>` from A on an interactive terminal (no `--yes`, no `--allow-first-sync`). A first-sync "target will be overwritten" warning appears and asks for confirmation. The warning's "In scope" block is built from the FolderSync job's own self-description — it names no transport mechanism directly (no hardcoded "rsync --delete" leaking from the orchestrator). Answer NO. The sync aborts with exactly one calm yellow "Sync aborted: ..." line — no red "Sync failed", no duplicate CRITICAL log line, logged at WARNING not CRITICAL. The terminal shows one continuous Live region throughout: no duplicate stacked "Recent Logs" panels, no skipped step number. B is not modified (no files transferred); A's sync-history is not written.
39	
40	why_human: Interactive TTY behavior + Rich Live terminal cursor rendering; the fix is unit-tested at the mechanism level (Live-instance reuse, single WARNING log, single CLI message) but not observed on a real terminal since these commits landed.
41	
42	result: pass
43	
44	note: Ran on pc1 → freshly-reset pc2, answered n. Core abort correct: single [WARNING] "Sync aborted by user", one calm yellow "Sync aborted: ..." CLI line, no red "Sync failed", no double-CRITICAL, no flooding, step counter 1→2→3 (no skip). Residual TUI artifact (accepted as pass, logged as minor gap): two stacked "Recent Logs" panels remain and the First Sync warning panel is truncated where the resumed Connection status line overwrote it — the same class as the original Test-2 complaint. 01-17 fixed flooding/step-skip/double-CRITICAL but not this confirm-boundary duplication.

47	
48	expected: Re-run `pc-switcher sync <B>` from A. The same first-sync warning appears. Answer YES. The sync proceeds: pc-switcher installs/updates on B, config syncs, the folder transfer runs, and B ends up matching A. A's sync-history records A as source, B as target.

Sync completed; pc-switcher installed on pc2 (0.1.0a14 via release-floor fallback), config applied, /home + /root transferred. Data-level copy/delete verified hands-on via planted fixtures + a 2nd (consecutive) sync: new files/nested dirs copied to pc2, executable perms preserved, symlink preserved as a symlink, and pc2-only files/dirs removed by rsync --delete. Findings from this test logged in Gaps (all minor): dry-run fidelity + log pointer, disk_space_monitor 0% bar, folder_sync 2% bar, install_on_target noisy WARNING, config-prompt decline aborts whole sync, and orchestrator dry-run-preview wording (job-specific "deleted" + log pointer).
55	
56	### 4. Clean round-trip alternation (whole-flow walkthrough)
57	
58	expected: Edit a file under the synced tree on A, then `pc-switcher sync <B>` — change lands on B. Edit a file on B, then `pc-switcher sync <A>` from B — change lands on A. Edit again on A, then `pc-switcher sync <B>`. A clean A→B / B→A / A→B alternation proceeds each time WITHOUT any out-of-order/consecutive-push warning (the clean case is silent). The live display stays coherent across all three runs — this is the scenario most likely to reproduce (or definitively disprove) the original flooding bug under sustained job activity. This is the maintainer's confidence pass over the whole feature with real edits.
59	
60	why_human: End-to-end confidence walkthrough on a real machine pair with real edits and real timing.

Data alternation A→B (Test 3) and B→A verified in both directions. Cosmetic finding logged: config_sync pauses/resumes the Live unconditionally on every interactive sync, leaving a stale 'Recent Logs' panel even with no prompt.

66	### 5. Consecutive-push heads-up — leave it waiting (interactive)
67	
68	expected: Create a consecutive-push topology — e.g. from B, `pc-switcher sync <A>` twice in a row without A pushing back in between. The second sync surfaces the out-of-order / consecutive-push heads-up and waits at the confirmation prompt. Leave this sync sitting at the prompt (do not answer yet) for the next test.
69	
70	why_human: Interactive prompt; the "waiting for the human" state is itself the UX being validated, and it sets up the concurrent-lock test.
71

74	note: From pc2, a 2nd consecutive pc2→pc1 surfaced the W3 "Consecutive Sync — No Back-Sync Received" heads-up and waited patiently at the prompt (held the lock). Answering y afterward completed the sync normally.

76	### 6. Concurrent sync blocked + how-to-unblock, then confirm the waiting one
77	
78	expected: While test 5's sync is still waiting at its prompt (and therefore holding B's lock), launch a SECOND `pc-switcher sync <A>` from B in another terminal. It fails fast (no hang) with "This machine is already involved in a sync (held by: …)" and how-to-unblock guidance (wait for the other sync; the lock auto-releases when its process exits; force-clear a genuinely stuck lock by terminating the holder, not by deleting the lock file). Then return to test 5's sync and answer YES; it completes normally. A follow-up sync afterwards succeeds.

80	why_human: Requires two concurrent interactive invocations racing on the same machine; the messaging/timing UX is not covered by the automated single-conflict test (which pre-holds the lock out-of-band).

Returning to Test 5's sync and answering y completed normally. Minor finding logged: that lock-conflict message prints twice (CRITICAL log + red CLI line) — user flagged very low prio.

7823112 feat(01): resurface run warnings via status-bar counter and end-of-run summary
Warnings scrolled past unread in the rolling Recent Logs panel. Capture every
>=WARNING record into a UI-owned buffer, surfaced two ways: a persistent "⚠ N"
counter in the status bar (part of the render, so pauses/scrolls never lose it)
and a static block reprinted into scrollback after the Live stops — on success
as well as failure — ending with a pc-switcher logs pointer.

WarningCaptureHandler (level WARNING) tees records synchronously on the listener
thread, so the buffer is complete once logging is flushed. Installed only in the
interactive path; the pcswitcher logger floor is lowered to include WARNING so
warnings are captured even when the TUI display level is set higher. Summary
lines are wrapped in Text to render arbitrary log content literally (no
MarkupError). Counter refresh is marshalled to the loop thread — Rich Live
redraws its stored renderable on auto-refresh rather than re-calling _render().

1	# Roadmap: PC-switcher
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	The orchestration framework is already built (Job architecture, self-installing orchestrator, btrfs snapshot safety, config, logging, locking, terminal UI, three-tier testing, CI). What remains is the actual sync work: a series of self-contained modular sync Jobs layered on that framework. The journey starts with the smallest vertical slice that proves the end-to-end workflow — replicating user data between two machines and verifying it with a bidirectional round-trip (Phase 1) — then broadens scope job by job (packages, system config, Docker, VMs, k3s) until the machine is a near-complete replica, and closes with rollback so any sync is reversible.
6	
7	## Milestones
8	
9	- Foundation (Complete) — Core framework, testing, logging, CI. Shipped before GSD adoption.
10	- Milestone 1: Home-Sync MVP (current focus) — Phase 1. The first working sync job, proven by a bidirectional round-trip integration test.
11	- Milestone 2: Full System-State Replication (planned) — Phases 2-6. Packages, system/app config, Docker, VMs, k3s.
12	- Milestone 3: Reliability Hardening (planned) — Phase 7. Rollback from pre-sync snapshots.
13	
14	## Phases
15	
16	**Phase Numbering:**
17	
18	- Integer phases (1, 2, 3): Planned milestone work.
19	- Decimal phases (2.1, 2.2): Urgent insertions (marked INSERTED), appearing between their surrounding integers in numeric order.
20	
21	- [x] **Foundation: Core framework, testing, logging, CI** — Complete (pre-GSD; specs 001-004)
22	- [x] **Phase 1: Home-Sync MVP (User Data Sync)** — Single-command replication of configured folders (`/home` and `/root` by default) over rsync-over-SSH, per-folder exclusions and metadata preserved, proven by a bidirectional round-trip (completed 2026-06-30)
23	- [ ] **Phase 2: Package Management Sync** — Replicate apt/snap/flatpak/.deb/PPA/script packages with conflict and version-mismatch detection
24	- [ ] **Phase 3: System & Application Configuration Sync** — Replicate GNOME, cloud mounts, systemd, `/etc`, users/groups with conflicting-change detection
25	- [ ] **Phase 4: Docker State Sync** — Replicate images, containers, volumes, and cache with running-container / incompatible-state detection
26	- [ ] **Phase 5: VM State Sync** — Replicate KVM/virt-manager VMs with a powered-off guard and concurrent-use detection
27	- [ ] **Phase 6: k3s Cluster Sync** — Replicate single-node k3s state including PVCs with cluster validation and active-workload detection
28	- [ ] **Phase 7: Rollback Capability** — Restore a machine to its pre-sync state from automatic btrfs snapshots via `pc-switcher rollback`
29	
30	## Phase Details
31	
32	### Foundation (Complete)
33	
34	**Goal**: Provide the orchestration framework every sync job builds on — Job architecture, self-installing orchestrator, btrfs snapshot safety, config, logging, locking, terminal UI, three-tier testing, and CI.
35	**Status**: Complete (shipped pre-GSD via specs 001-core, 002-testing-framework, 003-core-tests, 004-python-logging; living authority in `docs/system/`).
36	**Requirements**: REQ-core-001-infrastructure, REQ-testing-framework-002, REQ-core-tests-003, REQ-python-logging-004, REQ-feature-modular-architecture, REQ-environment-constraints
37	**Note**: Not a plannable GSD phase. Listed so the roadmap reflects what already exists; sync phases extend this framework rather than rebuild it.
38	
39	### Phase 1: Home-Sync MVP (User Data Sync)
40	
41	**Goal**: A user can run one command to replicate configured folders (`/home` and `/root` by default) from the source machine to the target over rsync-over-SSH, with machine-specific items excluded and file metadata preserved — and the result is proven correct in both directions. The job is a generic folder-sync mechanism (per-folder include/exclude), usable for any path; `/root` is included because rsync must run as root anyway to preserve cross-owner files. (`/etc` and other system config remain Phase 3.)
42	**Depends on**: Foundation
43	**Requirements**: REQ-sync-scope-user-data, REQ-machine-specific-exclusions, REQ-sync-scope-file-metadata, REQ-manual-sync-workflow, REQ-terminal-ux
44	**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
45	
46	  1. Running `pc-switcher sync <target>` on machine A copies the configured folders (`/home` and `/root` by default) to machine B; after sync, every included file exists on B and is byte-identical to A (verified by checksum).
47	  2. File metadata is preserved: owner, group, permissions, POSIX ACLs, and modification timestamps on B match A for every synced file.
48	  3. Machine-specific items are never copied — SSH private keys (`.ssh/id_*`), Tailscale config (`.config/tailscale`), GPU/shader and fontconfig caches, and excluded browser/IDE caches (VS Code) are absent from B's synced tree — while dev-tool caches (uv, pip, cargo, npm) ARE included.
49	  4. The sync is reversible and exclusions hold both directions: after modifying, adding, and deleting files on B and running `pc-switcher sync <A>` from B, A reflects B's changes byte-identically with metadata preserved, and the same machine-specific exclusions are honored on this reverse sync.
50	  5. A VM-isolated integration test automates the full A→B, mutate-on-B, B→A round-trip and asserts criteria 1-4 — the developer-facing milestone success metric.
51	
52	**Plans**: 18/18 plans complete

94	### Phase 2: Package Management Sync
95	
96	**Goal**: A user can replicate installed packages from source to target across all package sources, with conflicts and version mismatches detected and reported rather than silently overwritten.
97	**Depends on**: Phase 1
98	**Requirements**: REQ-sync-scope-packages, REQ-conflict-detection-no-resolution
99	**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
100	
101	  1. After sync, the target has the same apt, snap, and flatpak packages installed as the source (verifiable by querying each package manager).
102	  2. Manually-installed .debs, custom PPAs, and install-script-sourced packages are reproduced on the target.
103	  3. Package conflicts and version mismatches between source and target are detected and reported before any destructive change; machine-specific packages are not forced onto the target.
104	
105	**Plans**: TBD

107	### Phase 3: System & Application Configuration Sync
108	
109	**Goal**: A user can replicate machine-independent application and system configuration from source to target, with conflicting system changes detected.
110	**Depends on**: Phase 2
111	**Requirements**: REQ-sync-scope-app-and-system-config
112	**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
113	
114	  1. After sync, GNOME desktop configuration, cloud mounts, and systemd service definitions on the target match the source.
115	  2. Machine-independent system config (`/etc` entries, startup services, users/groups) is replicated while machine-specific config is excluded.
116	  3. Conflicting system changes between the machines are detected and reported rather than silently overwritten.
117	
118	**Plans**: TBD

121	
122	**Goal**: A user can replicate Docker state from source to target, with running containers or incompatible states detected so an unsafe sync is blocked.
123	**Depends on**: Phase 3
124	**Requirements**: REQ-sync-scope-docker
125	**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
126	
127	  1. After sync, the target has the same Docker images, container definitions, and named volumes as the source.
128	  2. Docker build cache / layer data is reproduced so target builds start warm.
129	  3. Running containers or incompatible Docker states are detected and reported, blocking an unsafe sync.
130	
131	**Plans**: TBD
132	
133	### Phase 5: VM State Sync
134	
135	**Goal**: A user can replicate KVM/virt-manager VMs from source to target, with a guard that only suspended/powered-off VMs are synced and concurrent VM usage is detected.
136	**Depends on**: Phase 4
137	**Requirements**: REQ-sync-scope-vms
138	**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
139	
140	  1. After sync, KVM/virt-manager VM definitions and disk images on the target match the source and boot correctly.
141	  2. A sync is refused, with a clear report, when a VM is running or in use on either machine; only suspended or powered-off VMs are synced.
142	  3. VM metadata and storage are preserved so the migrated VM resumes or runs on the target without manual repair.

## Phase 01 — Home-Sync MVP (user-data folder sync)

Implements the Home-Sync MVP: one command replicates configured folders (`/home` and `/root` by default) source→target over rsync-over-SSH, with machine-specific exclusions and full metadata preservation, proven correct in both directions.

This PR is opened **ready (non-draft) to run the VM integration tests in CI** — all offline checks already pass; the round-trip integration suite requires the Hetzner pc1/pc2 VMs that CI provisions.

## What's included

- `FolderSyncJob`: config-driven per-folder include/exclude, `validate()` preflight (sudo rsync / acl / folder existence), and `execute()` data plane (rsync flags per ADR-013, root-via-sudo, async subprocess over its own SSH transport, Rich TUI progress + six-level logging, dry-run preview).
- `folder_sync` config surface + defaults (`/home`, `/root`, machine-specific exclusions; dev-tool caches kept).
- Unified dry-run contract (ADR-014): no file writes, no history updates in dry-run.
- Topology-based sync-safety model (ADR-015 — see pivot below).
- VM-isolated A→B / mutate-on-B / B→A round-trip integration test.

## Divergence-safety design pivot (ADR-015)

Late in the phase, the original btrfs content-based target-divergence guard was **removed** and replaced with a topology-based safety model. The old guard had two critical bugs — CR-01 (unanchored tool-state filter false-negative → data-loss path) and CR-02 (Phase-7 install artifacts false-positive on every upgrade+sync). Rather than patch the content-diffing approach, ADR-015 eliminates it:

- Sync-history is now a simple `{last_role, last_peer}` store (btrfs per-target generation store deleted).
- The orchestrator runs a single `_check_out_of_order()` step after the target lock, reading both machines' history and warning/confirming only when the target may hold independent state (W1/W2/W3 truth table).
- One override flag `--allow-out-of-order` replaces `--allow-divergence` / `--allow-consecutive`.

## Verification status

- `uv run basedpyright` → 0 errors · `uv run ruff check .` → clean
- `uv run pytest` → 508 passed (integration tests deselected offline — need VMs)

To summarize where things stand:

- **Auto-merge is enabled** on PR #160 (merge commit, no squash/no-ff, per your convention). It will merge into `main` automatically the moment the Integration Tests check goes green, and won't merge if it fails.
- The **VM integration suite is running now** (~13 min typical). I'm watching it in the background and will report when it finishes and the merge lands.
- All other required/optional checks already pass (CI, Lint, Unit, issue-closing keyword).

Nothing more to do until that run completes. If it comes back red, I'll surface the failure instead of merging. If you'd rather I *not* auto-merge and instead wait for your explicit go-ahead after CI is green, say so and I'll disable it.

One heads-up for after the merge: this PR completes **Milestone 1 (Home-Sync MVP)**. Once it's on `main`, the natural next GSD steps are `/gsd-complete-milestone` (archive the milestone) and then Phase 2 planning — both want a fresh `/clear`.
First, you said "Next Up Phase 2: Package Management Sync" Now you say "PR completes Milestone 1; do /gsd-complete-milestone". What is it?

You're right to flag it — my second message was wrong. **Phase 2 is the correct next step.** Do *not* run `/gsd-complete-milestone`.

The confusion is two different meanings of "milestone":

- **ROADMAP.md prose** groups the phases into three narrative milestones: "Milestone 1: Home-Sync MVP" = Phase 1 only. That's the label I latched onto for the "completes Milestone 1" remark.
- **GSD's tracked milestone** is what actually drives the workflow — and there it's a single milestone `v1.0` spanning **all 7 phases**. STATE.md says `milestone: v1.0`, `total_phases: 7`, and `phase.complete` returned `is_last_phase: false`, `next_phase: 2`.

So as far as GSD state is concerned, no milestone finished — only Phase 1 of 7. `/gsd-complete-milestone` archives a *GSD* milestone and only applies when `is_last_phase: true` (after Phase 7). Running it now would be premature.

Correct next step, consistent with the transition output:

> `/clear` then `/gsd-plan-phase 2` (or `/gsd-discuss-phase 2` first) — **Phase 2: Package Management Sync**

The narrative "Milestone 1 done" is true in the human/README sense (the MVP slice works and is about to hit `main`), but it's not a GSD milestone boundary. Scratch the `/gsd-complete-milestone` suggestion entirely.

```markdown

- [Major achievement 4]

- [X] files created/modified
- [Y] lines of code (primary language)

**What's next:** [Brief description of next milestone goals, or "Project complete"]

```

<structure>
If MILESTONES.md doesn't exist, create it with header:

```markdown
# Project Milestones: [Project Name]

[Entries in reverse chronological order - newest first]
```
</structure>

<guidelines>
**When to create milestones:**
- Initial v1.0 MVP shipped
- Major version releases (v2.0, v3.0)
- Significant feature milestones (v1.1, v1.2)
- Before archiving planning (capture what was shipped)

**Don't create milestones for:**
- Individual phase completions (normal workflow)
- Work in progress (wait until shipped)
- Minor bug fixes that don't constitute a release

**Stats to include:**
- Count modified files: `git diff --stat feat(XX-XX)..feat(YY-YY) | tail -1`
- Count LOC: `find . -name "*.swift" -o -name "*.ts" | xargs wc -l` (or relevant extension)
- Phase/plan/task counts from ROADMAP
- Timeline from first phase commit to last phase commit

**Git range format:**
- First commit of milestone → last commit of milestone
- Example: `feat(01-01)` → `feat(04-01)` for phases 1-4

365	**Synthesizer output self-heal (#222) — verify SUMMARY.md materialized:** The synthesizer's canonical output is `.planning/research/SUMMARY.md` on disk; its brief structured return (`## SYNTHESIS COMPLETE` plus a few `###` confirmation lines) is NOT the file content. A known LLM false-refusal (issue #222) sometimes makes the agent return the full SUMMARY.md document inline — fabricating a write restriction (e.g. "the runtime is blocking file writes") — instead of writing the file. Prompt hardening alone does not fully eliminate it, so the orchestrator MUST absorb the failure deterministically before spawning `gsd-roadmapper`:
366	
367	1. Verify `.planning/research/SUMMARY.md` exists AND is substantive — non-empty, and free of any leftover `<!-- gsd:write-continue -->` continuation sentinel (which marks a truncated/incomplete write). You may validate with `gsd-tools verify-summary .planning/research/SUMMARY.md` — it exits 0 regardless, so check its JSON `passed` field (`"passed": false` means missing or invalid), not the process exit code. If it passes, continue normally.
368	2. If it is MISSING or invalid AND the synthesizer's return message contains the FULL SUMMARY.md document — recognizable by the template's top-level markers `# Project Research Summary`, `## Key Findings`, `## Implications for Roadmap`, and `## Sources`, not merely the brief `## SYNTHESIS COMPLETE` confirmation — the false-refusal fired: write that returned document to `.planning/research/SUMMARY.md` with the Write tool, then commit ALL research artifacts the synthesizer owns (it commits on behalf of the four researchers) with `gsd-tools query commit "docs: complete project research" --files .planning/research/` unless they are already committed. Log `⚠ #222 self-heal: synthesizer returned SUMMARY.md inline without writing it; orchestrator persisted the file.`
369	3. If it is MISSING or invalid AND the return is only a brief confirmation (no full SUMMARY document to recover), the synthesizer genuinely failed — surface the error and stop; do NOT spawn `gsd-roadmapper` against a missing or incomplete SUMMARY.md.
370	
371	This guarantees `gsd-roadmapper` (which lists SUMMARY.md as required reading) never runs against a missing or truncated SUMMARY.md.

content.text:
I read the `/gsd-new-milestone` workflow end to end. Here's what it actually does — which settles it:

- It creates/advances to **one** milestone at a time. The roadmapper it spawns derives phases **"from THIS MILESTONE's requirements only"** and writes a ROADMAP with just `## Overview`, `## Phases`, `## Phase Details`, `## Progress`. It does **not** emit a `## Milestones` section — that section isn't a tool artifact at all.
- STATE.md tracks the single current milestone. Shipped milestones are recorded separately in `MILESTONES.md` (history).
- On your numbering point: GSD **continues** phase numbers across milestones by default (v1.0 ends at phase 5 → v1.1 starts at phase 6). Restarting at 1 is opt-in via `--reset-phase-numbers`. So "each milestone numbered from 1" is available but not the default.

So the doc is malformed: someone packed **three milestones of scope into one milestone's roadmap** and added a hand-written `## Milestones` section the tooling never produces. That's the confusion you spotted. (Likely `/gsd-new-project` at bootstrap, but the single-milestone model is identical either way.)

The correction depends on one product decision that's genuinely yours — I'm not guessing it a third time:

10	**Precondition — never cut a release until CI is green on that exact commit.** Push first, wait for the push/PR CI (incl. the integration suite) to pass on the commit's SHA, and only then create the tag + release. A release is a claim the code is healthy; publishing seconds after a push (before any run finishes) ships unverified code and also races the version-match integration tests (they compare a checkout-time `git describe` version against the live "latest release floor" at install time, so a release published mid-run makes the target install a newer version than the source describes → `assert '0.1.0-alpha.N' in '...alpha.N+1'`). If a release is genuinely needed on an in-flight run, treat that run as void and re-run after the tag is stable.
11	
12	Releasing pc-switcher (for install / install-on-target to resolve the correct version) requires an **annotated** git tag. `uv-dynamic-versioning` (dunamai via `git describe`) **ignores lightweight tags** and falls back to the nearest annotated tag, yielding a wrong dev-suffixed version (e.g. `0.1.0-alpha.12+post.31.dev.0.<sha>`).
13	
14	**Why:** the repo has constraints that make the obvious paths fail:
15	- Plain `gh release create <tag>` creates a **lightweight** tag → wrong version.
16	- Direct `git push` of a tag is **blocked by a tag-creation ruleset** ("creations being restricted").
17	- **Immutable releases** are on: deleting a release with `--cleanup-tag` **burns the tag name** (cannot be reused — must bump to the next version).
18	
19	**How to apply:** create the annotated tag via the GitHub git data API, then the release:
20	```bash
21	TAG_OBJ=$(gh api repos/flaksit/pc-switcher/git/tags -f tag=vX -f message="vX" -f object=<sha> -f type=commit --jq '.sha')
22	gh api repos/flaksit/pc-switcher/git/refs -f ref=refs/tags/vX -f sha="$TAG_OBJ"
23	gh release create vX --prerelease --verify-tag --title "vX" --notes "..."
24	```
25	Then verify with a FRESH uv cache (`UV_CACHE_DIR=<tmp> uv pip install "git+…@vX"`), because uv's git cache can mask a new tag on a machine that already cached the underlying commit (fresh/reset machines are unaffected). Improvement tracked in issue #161 (make standard `gh release create` work). Claude cuts the releases in this repo, not the user. Related: [[uat-give-instructions-not-execute]].

First real release — **Phase 1: Home-Sync MVP**.

One command replicates configured folders (`/home` and `/root` by default) from a source machine to a target over rsync-over-SSH, proven correct by a bidirectional round-trip.

## Highlights

- **Folder sync** — generic per-folder include/exclude; byte-identical content with full metadata preserved (owner, group, permissions, POSIX ACLs, mtime, hard/symlinks), rsync running as root via sudo.
- **Machine-specific exclusions** — SSH private keys, Tailscale config, GPU/shader and fontconfig caches, and VS Code caches are never copied; dev-tool caches (uv, pip, cargo, npm) are kept.
- **Topology-based sync safety (ADR-015)** — first-sync overwrite warning, out-of-order / consecutive-push heads-up, and a concurrent-sync lock with how-to-unblock guidance.
- **Self-installing orchestrator** — installs/updates pc-switcher and syncs config to the target; automatic btrfs pre-sync snapshots; `--dry-run` preview.
- **Terminal UX** — single live progress panel; run warnings resurfaced via a persistent counter and an end-of-run summary pointing to `pc-switcher logs`.

## Verification

Full VM-isolated integration suite green (57/57): A→B, mutate-on-B, B→A round-trip asserting content, metadata, ACLs, and exclusions in both directions.
